title: Meatballs in Marinara Sauce

overview.description: Tender beef and pork meatballs gently simmered in a simple tomato, garlic and herb marinara sauce.

method: Place the breadcrumbs in a bowl and pour over the milk.
Leave for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the beef mince, pork mince, egg, onion, garlic, Parmesan, parsley, oregano, salt and black pepper.
Mix gently until just combined.
Do not overwork the mixture.
Form into approximately 24 meatballs.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan or casserole over medium-high heat.
Brown the meatballs in batches, turning regularly.
Remove the meatballs once browned. They do not need to be fully cooked at this stage.
For the sauce, add the onion to the pan and cook gently for 5–8 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, oregano, basil and water.
Season with salt and black pepper.
Add the sugar if the tomatoes are particularly acidic.
Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.
Return the meatballs to the sauce.
Cover and simmer gently for 25–30 minutes until the meatballs are completely cooked through.
Taste and adjust the seasoning.
Serve with pasta, crusty bread or in sandwiches.

learning.did-you-know: Soaking breadcrumbs in milk creates a panade, which helps meatballs retain moisture and keeps them tender rather than dense.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make tender meatballs and how to develop flavour by browning the meat before finishing it in a tomato sauce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A flexible family meal that works with pasta, bread or sandwiches and produces excellent freezer-friendly leftovers.
chapman-tips: Don't overwork the meat mixture or the meatballs may become dense.
Soaking the breadcrumbs in milk helps keep the meatballs tender.
Brown the meatballs in batches so they actually colour rather than steam.
The meatballs will finish cooking in the sauce.
Keep the sauce at a gentle simmer rather than a vigorous boil.
The beef and pork combination gives a good balance of flavour and tenderness.
For sandwiches, use slightly less sauce so the bread does not become soggy.
Melted mozzarella makes an excellent addition to meatball sandwiches.
Make smaller meatballs for sandwiches and larger ones for serving with pasta.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Freeze the meatballs in their sauce in meal-sized portions.
notes.leftovers: Excellent. Use in pasta, meatball sandwiches, wraps or with crusty bread.

similar-recipes: Beef Stir-Fry with Honey Pepper Sauce
